surfaced_from: PWA request 2026-05-25 05:19 UTC — CISSP-like CTO security audit and prioritization
capability_needed: Replace URL query-token PWA auth with cookie/session auth and reduce token logging risk
details: The PWA accepts the auth token from the URL query string. Query tokens are easy to leak through browser history, screenshots, copied links, Caddy/access logs, and referrer-like surfaces. The Caddy config writes structured access logs; even with sensible headers, query-string auth is not an acceptable long-term control for CTO.
search_trail: Ran openclaw status and openclaw security audit --deep on cto-v1; result: 0 critical, 1 warn, 1 info; gateway bound to 127.0.0.1 with token auth.
Ran ss -ltnup on cto-v1; found public SSH plus public HTTP/HTTPS and loopback-only OpenClaw/Hermes/A2A services.
Queried Hetzner API for servers, primary IPs, and firewalls; found no cloud firewalls and multiple public CTO servers/candidates.
Inspected /etc/caddy/Caddyfile without secrets; cto.husband.llc reverse-proxies to 127.0.0.1:8088 with security headers and access logging.
Scanned git history/current tree for secret-shaped patterns and key material; confirmed historical VAPID private key material and multiple documented credential-handling risks.
Checked file permissions for /opt/cto, ~/.openclaw, ~/.hermes, ~/.codex; sensitive home config dirs are mostly 0600/0700, while project files are group-writable by cto group.
Checked unattended-upgrades; service is enabled and active.
Attempted privileged sshd/fail2ban verification; elevated execution is unavailable in this PWA runtime, so privileged SSH posture remains unverified.
proposed_resolution: Move PWA auth to an HttpOnly Secure SameSite cookie or equivalent session exchange, redact access logs, expire old query tokens, and add replay/session invalidation.
blast_radius: Changing auth can temporarily lock John out of the PWA or break background jobs that still use bearer/query token assumptions.
rollback_plan: Keep a temporary one-time bootstrap path, verify login from John’s device, then disable query-token auth.
resolution_notes: Code repair is implemented: PWA auth now uses signed HttpOnly/SameSite session cookies; API query-token auth is rejected; the PWA shell is not public when auth is configured; legacy query-token access logs and operational artifacts are redacted. Verification artifact: logs/security/BACKLOG-009-query-token-auth-reconciliation-2026-05-26T2000Z.md. Remaining live token rotation/session invalidation overlaps BACKLOG-013 and BACKLOG-006 and must be coordinated to avoid locking John out. 2026-05-26T21:32Z: live PWA auth was restored after a failed rotation path left PWA_AUTH_TOKEN blank in /opt/cto/.env; unauthenticated root/API/query-token checks returned 401 and safe security gates passed. Token value was not recorded.
escalation_log: Priority raised to P0 on 2026-05-26 per John: 'the pwa is worthless as is. raise all pwa issues to p0.'
resolution_log: 2026-05-26T21:25Z: PWA_AUTH_TOKEN rotated by Claude Code per John's 'Rotate' directive. New bootstrap URL delivered in-conversation. See CTO-DECISION-018.
